title: Chen-Chvatal Conjecture
statement_text: Every finite metric space $M = (V, d)$ satisfies the de Bruijn-Erd\H{o}s property: $M$ has a universal line, or at least $|V|$ distinct lines.
context_text: Chen and Chv\'atal posed this in 2008 as Question 1 of [5], asking whether all finite metric spaces satisfy the de Bruijn-Erd\H{o}s property (DBE). By lack of a counterexample the question has since grown into a conjecture; it remains open even for graph metrics.
paper_contribution: Proves that graph metrics induced by \{house, hole\}-free graphs (graphs with no induced house and no induced cycle of length at least 5) satisfy the de Bruijn-Erd\H{o}s property, thereby resolving Problem 3 of Chv\'atal's 2018 survey.
paper_title: Graphs with no induced house nor induced hole have the de Bruijn-Erdős property
